This repository demonstrates the performance power of NumPy's vectorized operations compared to standard Python for-loops. It serves as a benchmark for understanding why NumPy is the backbone of high-performance computing in Python.  🚀 The Concept Standard Python loops process elements one by one at the interpreter level, which introduces significant overhead. NumPy, however, uses Vectorization, allowing it to perform operations on entire arrays at once by leveraging highly optimized C and Fortran code under the hood.  📊 Benchmark Results In this experiment, we perform element-wise addition on two arrays, each containing 1 million random numbers.  Typical results on a modern machine:  Standard Loop: ~0.1500 seconds  Vectorized Operation: ~0.0015 seconds  Performance Boost: ~100x speedup  🛠️ Code Snippet The core logic compares the execution time between a manual for loop and the NumPy + operator:
